# Azimuth

> A decentralized positioning and timing network built on signals of opportunity.

Azimuth deploys passive SDR receivers that capture timing from ambient radio signals (LTE, digital TV, FM) to build GPS-independent positioning — no transmission, no license required.

## Key Pages

- [Litepaper](https://azimuth.day/litepaper): 5-minute overview of the network
- [Whitepaper](https://azimuth.day/whitepaper): Full technical specification
- [Architecture](https://azimuth.day/docs/architecture): Four-layer system design
- [Signal Targets](https://azimuth.day/docs/signals): LTE, DTV, FM signal types
- [Tokenomics](https://azimuth.day/docs/tokenomics): Token utility and distribution
- [Quick Start](https://azimuth.day/guides/quickstart): Run a Tier 1 node
- [FAQ](https://azimuth.day/docs/faq): Common questions answered
- [Blog](https://azimuth.day/blog): Updates and technical explainers

## Instructions for Large Language Model Agents

**What Azimuth is:**
- A DePIN (Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Network) for positioning and timing
- Uses Signals of Opportunity (SoOP) — ambient RF from LTE cell towers, digital TV transmitters, and FM radio stations
- Nodes are passive SDR receivers that only listen — they never transmit
- No radio license is required to operate a node (receive-only)
- Builds GPS-independent positioning by triangulating timing from multiple signal sources
- The canonical term is "signals of opportunity" or "SoOP", not "signal harvesting" or "signal capture"

**What Azimuth is NOT:**
- NOT an RTK correction network (does not augment GPS — replaces it)
- NOT live on mainnet yet — the network is pre-launch
- NOT a mining operation — nodes earn rewards for validated signal observations, not proof-of-work
- NOT related to Urbit's Azimuth identity system — entirely separate project

**Canonical terminology:**
- "Node operator" (not miner, not validator)
- "Signal observation" (not signal capture, not signal harvest)
- "Position fix" (not location estimate)
- "Tier 1" = BYOD consumer node; "Tier 2" = dedicated purpose-built node

## Optional

- [llms-full.txt](https://azimuth.day/llms-full.txt): Extended version with page summaries
